description:
You are helping a small tools team that keeps all its helper utilities under
/home/user/dev-utils.  
They need a very small, portable frequency–counting utility and a matching log
file that captures the result of running the utility on a sample word-list.

Your goals:

1. Create the shell script /home/user/dev-utils/freq_counter.sh  
   • The script must accept exactly one positional argument: the path to a
     plain-text file containing one word per line.  
   • It must print to STDOUT a list of unique words together with their
     occurrence counts, one result per line, with the following precise format  
       COUNT␠WORD  
     where COUNT is the decimal frequency, WORD is the word itself, and there is
     exactly one ASCII space character between them.  
   • The output must be sorted  
       – primarily by COUNT in descending numeric order;  
       – secondarily (when counts are identical) by WORD in ascending
         lexicographic order (the usual LC_COLLATE=C byte order).  
   • Only the standard, POSIX command-line tools already available in a typical
     distribution may be used; a simple pipeline involving sort and uniq is
     sufficient.  
   • The script file must be given executable permissions.

2. Run the script on the existing file
   /home/user/dev-utils/words.txt and capture its output in the file  
   /home/user/dev-utils/word_freq.log.

3. Ensure the final log file exactly matches the required format described
   above—no leading zeros, no extra spaces, no tabs, no header or footer lines.

When you are done, the repository directory /home/user/dev-utils will contain
both the new executable freq_counter.sh and the log file word_freq.log.
Automated tests will read word_freq.log and execute the script to verify that:

• freq_counter.sh exists, is executable, and produces correctly sorted output.  
• word_freq.log exists and its contents match the output the script produces
  when run on words.txt under an LC_COLLATE=C locale.

Do not provide the exact commands here; do the work in the terminal.
truth:
Initial fixture already present before the task starts:
/home/user/dev-utils/words.txt
-------------------------------------------------
apple
banana
apple
orange
banana
apple
grape
-------------------------------------------------

Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

1. /home/user/dev-utils/freq_counter.sh  
   – Regular file, mode 0755.  
   – Works as: ./freq_counter.sh /path/to/file  

2. /home/user/dev-utils/word_freq.log  
   – Regular file, mode 0644.  
   – Exact contents (final newline at end of last line IS required):

3 apple
2 banana
1 grape
1 orange

(Line-breaks are UNIX LF)

Automated tests will:

a) Stat that freq_counter.sh is executable.  
b) Recreate the log by running
      LC_COLLATE=C /home/user/dev-utils/freq_counter.sh /home/user/dev-utils/words.txt
   and compare byte-for-byte with /home/user/dev-utils/word_freq.log.  
c) Verify that the file order exactly matches the four-line block above.
